pi_items.source: Consistent across task.json description, setup_task.sh:7, and export_result.sh:8

Confirmed in export_result.sh:97, task.json description mentions https://www.cnn.com, https://www.wikipedia.org, https://github.com. All three verified as real, publicly accessible websites via HTTP HEAD requests returning 200 OK status.

Code analysis shows export_result.sh:61 checks only 5 terms: ["scripting", "rendering", "painting", "loading", "idle"]. The units "ms" and "s" are not checked as standalone required terms; instead they are matched via timing regex pattern (\d+(\.\d+)?\s*(ms|s)\b) on export_result.sh:66. Microsoft Edge DevTools Performance panel documentation confirms these 5 performance categories exist, with timing shown primarily in milliseconds (ms).

The metadata value exists but is not validated anywhere in the verification logic. export_result.sh captures report_info["size"] on line 47, but verifier.py never checks this against the 200-byte minimum. The scoring system (verifier.py:49-144) has no reference to file size validation.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires profiling real websites (CNN, Wikipedia, and GitHub verified as publicly accessible) using Microsoft Edge's DevTools Performance panel. The report path is consistently specified as /home/ga/Desktop/performance_profile_report.txt across all scripts. The Performance panel reports five key metrics categories: Scripting, Rendering, Painting, Loading, and Idle. Timing values use milliseconds as the primary unit. The task operates on real data (actual website performance profiles) rather than synthetic data.
